seo.title: LangChain Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangChain agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using LangChain for AI-powered applications face stringent oversight under FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53, where any hallucination, infinite loop, or unexplainable output can lead to national security breaches or loss of public trust. Compliance guardrails are essential to ensure AI systems processing sensitive citizen data remain reliable, auditable, and aligned with Executive Order on AI mandates. Vex provides runtime verification for LangChain chains and agents, preventing common failure modes while generating audit trails for regulatory compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and authorization of cloud-based AI systems handling federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex wraps LangChain's chain.invoke() to log all inputs/outputs and detect anomalies in real-time, providing FedRAMP-compliant audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems, including controls for accuracy, explainability, and error prevention.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies outputs against ground truth for RAG chains and flags hallucinations, ensuring NIST-compliant reliability testing.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and safeguards for high-risk AI affecting rights or safety.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automates impact assessments by scoring chain reliability and generating explainability reports for government review.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible and consistently formatted for citizens with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces consistent output formatting across LangChain steps, preventing accessibility violations.

industryRisks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
industryRisks.description: RAG chains retrieving classified documents hallucinate false facts, leading to incorrect policy decisions.
industryRisks.example: Agent cites fabricated classified intel during crisis response briefing.

industryRisks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
industryRisks.description: Government agents enter infinite loops calling secure APIs, causing system outages.
industryRisks.example: Benefits approval agent loops on database queries, delaying citizen payments.

industryRisks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
industryRisks.description: Conversation chains overflow with case history, leaking PII or crashing during audits.
industryRisks.example: FOIA request chain exposes unrelated citizen data from token overflow.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
industryRisks.description: Multi-step chains produce malformed JSON for downstream government systems.
industryRisks.example: Visa processing chain outputs inconsistent schemas, blocking integration.

implementation.title: Wrap RAG Chain to Prevent Hallucinations
implementation.description: Verify RetrievalQA chain outputs against retrieved documents to catch hallucinations before citizen-facing responses.
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from langchain_openai import OpenAI
from vex import verify

# Government RAG chain for policy Q&A
qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(
    llm=OpenAI(temperature=0),
    chain_type="stuff",
    retriever=gov_docs_vectorstore.as_retriever()
)

# Vex verification prevents hallucinated policy advice
def safe_qa(query):
    result = verify(
        qa_chain.invoke,
        input={"query": query},
        checks=[
            "no_hallucination",  # Compares output to retrieved context
            "no_pii_leak",       # FISMA PII protection
            "json_schema"        # Section 508 formatting
        ]
    )
    return result

response = safe_qa("What are eligibility requirements for federal benefits?")

implementation.title: Prevent Agent Tool Call Loops
implementation.description: Guard government agents from infinite API loops when accessing secure databases.
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_openai_functions_agent, AgentExecutor
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from vex import verify

# Government benefits agent
llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4", temperature=0)
agent = create_openai_functions_agent(llm, tools=[benefits_db_tool, audit_log_tool])
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, max_iterations=5)

# Vex prevents FISMA-violating infinite loops
def safe_agent(input):
    return verify(
        agent_executor.invoke,
        input={"input": input},
        checks=[
            "no_infinite_loop",     # Detects tool call cycles
            "max_iterations_exceeded",
            "audit_trail"           # FedRAMP logging
        ],
        timeout=30000
    )

result = safe_agent("Process citizen ID 12345 for benefits approval")

implementation.title: TypeScript: Context Window Protection
implementation.description: Protect long FOIA conversations from token overflow and PII leaks using Vex SDK.

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangChain in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A federal agency deploys a LangChain-powered benefits processing system to handle 10 million citizen applications annually. The RAG agent retrieves eligibility rules from secure document stores while conversational chains maintain case history across multi-step approvals. During peak tax season, the system begins hallucinating eligibility requirements, incorrectly denying benefits to thousands of citizens and triggering congressional inquiries. Tool-calling agents enter infinite loops when querying legacy mainframe databases, causing 48-hour outages during critical welfare distribution periods.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex verification around chain.invoke(), hallucinations dropped 98% as outputs were cross-checked against retrieved documents, generating FISMA-compliant audit logs. Infinite loops were eliminated through runtime iteration limits and cycle detection, restoring 99.9% uptime. The system now produces explainable, Section 508-compliant outputs with full provenance tracing, passing FedRAMP reauthorization and avoiding contract debarment while rebuilding public trust.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with your LangChain applications today to achieve FedRAMP and NIST 800-53 compliance.
